THE ELDER OF THE EGYPTIANS' MONASTERY It was a distance of twelve miles. The same Stephan told us this about the same elder. One day some of his relatives came wanting Anazarbos is the metropolis of Cilicia Secunda. About twelve miles 10 see him, When they came into the Lavra, they enquired where his away is the so-called ‘Lavra of the Egyptians’. The fathers of that cell was. Some people showed them where it was located, so they place told us that, five years earlier, an elder named Julian had died went and knocked at the door. Realising who they were, the elder there. They testified that he spent about seventy years in one little prayed God not to let him be seen by them. He opened the door cave and that he possessed nothing of this world's goods other than and went out of his cell, but he was not seen by them. He went out a hair shirt, a cloak, a book of the gospels, and a wooden bowl. into the wilderness and did not return to his cell until they had gone They also said this of him: that all his life long he never lit a lamp away. to give light, for at night-time a light shone upon him from heaven sufficient for him to discern the sequence of the letters when he was 54.
